activation_barrier.note: quality-neutral in the batch regime; the barrier's contribution is liveness (annealing bounds stall, H5) and the infeasible and stalled semantics (H3), which the batch engine has no time axis to show

H2.claim: CTA quality is not worse than pull-based and within margin of the optimum

H4.claim: the integrity gate substantially reduces out-of-scope writes

H7.claim: self-reports over-predict realised success because they omit competence

H8.claim: the track-record correction recovers completion under miscalibration

H1.claim: peak per-node load grows more slowly for CTA than central

H2.claim: CTA quality is not worse than pull-based and within margin of the optimum

H6.claim: CTA advantage over the optimum increases with heterogeneity

H3.claim: the engine labels infeasible and stalled tasks correctly

H4.claim: the integrity gate substantially reduces out-of-scope writes

H5.claim: annealing bounds the stall time of feasible tasks

H7.claim: self-reports over-predict realised success because they omit competence

H8.claim: the track-record correction recovers completion under miscalibration

H9.claim: CTA matches or beats central coordination once its table is stale

H10.claim: the activation barrier routes each subtask to a correct specialist
